channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania to Host 13th African Financial Markets Forum in July 2026
item.description: Tanzania goin' host the 13th edition of the Building African Financial Markets (BAFM) Forum from 1st to 3rd July 2026, in Dar es Salaam. The forum dey organised by Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange (DSE) t...

item.title: Sharon Aruparayil’s ‘Mehendi Nights’ wins Asia regional award in 2026 Commonwealth Short Story Prize
item.description: The 2026 Commonwealth Short Story Prize has officially announced its regional winners, recognising outstanding literary talent across continents. From India, writer Sharon Aruparayil has been named th...

item.title: Three Dawson Creek Residents Recognized for Bravery During 2022 Bear Attack
item.description: Three residents of Dawson Creek, British Columbia, have received national honours for their courageous actions during a black bear attack in October 2022. Leosette Canoy Hansen received the Star of Co...

item.title: The Life and Art of Savely Sorin, Portraitist of the Elite
item.description: Savely Sorin (1878–1953) was a distinguished portrait painter whose work merged the formal traditions of the Russian Academy with the sophisticated cosmopolitan style of the early 20th century. Born i...

item.title: Australians to quarantine after hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
item.description: Four Australians, a New Zealander and a British national are currently in the Netherlands awaiting evacuation to Australia after a hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship. The Australian gov...

item.title: US-Iran Conflict Costs Exceed $29 Billion Amid Fragile Ceasefire
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran has now cost Washington over $29 billion since hostilities began in February 2026, according to Pentagon estimates. This increase comes as US in...

item.title: Discovery of a Homeric Fragment Inside a Roman-Era Egyptian Mummy
item.description: Archaeologists have uncovered a 1,600-year-old Roman-era Egyptian mummy containing an unexpected item: a fragment of Homer’s Iliad placed inside the abdomen. Initially, some reports suggested that the...

item.title: Durban entertainer admits to possession of child pornography, sentencing pending
item.description: Durban entertainer Anthony Stonier, aged 60, has pleaded guilty to possession of child pornography. His arrest occurred in April 2024 at his Glenwood flat during a joint operation by the SAPS Family V...

item.title: Kenya’s Gen Z Focus on Voter Registration to Influence 2027 Elections
item.description: After the heavy street protests in June 2024 failed to force President William Ruto to resign, Kenya’s Generation Z youths have changed their approach to politics. Now dem dey use voting as their main...

item.title: A Visual History of Ladies’ Home Journal Covers from the Early 20th Century
item.description: Founded in 1883, the Ladies’ Home Journal became one of the most influential magazines in American history, guiding women’s domestic, social, and cultural lives throughout the 20th century. At its hei...

item.title: Nationwide Chemist Shutdown on May 20 May Disrupt Medicine Supplies
item.description: On May 20, over 12.4 lakh chemists across India are planning a nationwide shutdown to protest the unregulated online sale of medicines and aggressive discounting by e-pharmacy platforms. The All India...

item.title: Flossie Rogers: A Story of Polio, Resilience, and Nursing Compassion
item.description: In June 1957, Flossie Rogers, a young woman affected by polio, experienced a small yet profound moment of dignity and hope when a nurse held up a mirror so she could see her own reflection. Confined t...

item.title: Enderun College Showcases Design Evolution Exhibit at S Maison
item.description: Enderun College’s College of Architecture & Design is currently showcasing a unique exhibit at S Maison that traces the evolution of design through the years. The exhibit features a curated collection...

item.title: Plans Considered to Open Unused Space at Kelowna General Hospital Amid Capacity Concerns
item.description: Kelowna General Hospital (KGH) may soon put an empty area of its Centennial Tower into use, according to a leaked memo circulated to hospital staff. The memo refers to the unfinished sixth floor, whic...

item.title: Woman released after 27 years on death row due to lost court records
item.description: Ms Rehema Ndoje, a woman from Mwitikila village in Bahi District, spent 27 years in Tanzanian prisons under a death sentence due to missing court records. She was first imprisoned on May 24, 1999, acc...

item.title: Nollywood Stars Mourn the Passing of Actor Alex Ekubo
item.description: The Nigerian entertainment industry don dey shake as news don break say Nollywood actor Alex Ekubo, popularly known as 'Ikuku', don pass away. His colleagues and friends dey share heartfelt tributes o...

item.title: Behavioural appeals alone cannot solve India’s economic challenges, say experts
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi recently urged Indian citizens to adopt austerity measures, such as reducing foreign travel, postponing gold purchases, using public transport, consuming less edible oil, ...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi raises objections over selection process of next CBI director
item.description: Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha, Rahul Gandhi, on Tuesday expressed strong dissent over the process of selecting the next Director of the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI). During a meeting of t...

item.title: Reader Discusses Work Ethic and Public Perception
item.description: This article features a letter to the editor in The Australian Financial Review, where a reader reflects on the challenges and public perception of working hard. The reader expresses concern about the...

item.title: B.C. shortens hantavirus quarantine as Canadian travel patterns to U.S. cities shift
item.description: On May 12, 2026, several key developments in Canadian news were highlighted. British Columbia has advised a 21-day isolation period for four Canadians returning from the hantavirus-exposed MV Hondius ...
